title: To waive certain provisions in the case of an emergency declaration under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act.
plain_summary: This bill lets Puerto Rico, Washington DC, American Samoa, and the US Virgin Islands buy emergency supplies from any country during disasters. Normally, the government has to follow "Buy American" rules that require buying things made in America. But when there's an emergency, these places can skip those rules and buy supplies wherever they can find them fastest. The head of FEMA would not make them follow the usual buying rules during emergencies.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Economic Development, Public Buildings, and Emergency Management.

sections.title: Section 1 Waiver of Certain Provisions
sections.text: When the President declares an emergency under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act, the head of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) must skip certain federal buying rules. These rules are found in chapter eighty-three of title forty-one of the United States Code and normally control how government agencies buy things and make contracts. FEMA will not require Puerto Rico, Washington DC, American Samoa, or the US Virgin Islands to follow these federal buying rules when they purchase supplies or hire contractors during the emergency.
